factors.name: AI Semiconductor Demand Surge
factors.summary: Samsung expected to post 18-fold profit jump on AI memory demand (per Bloomberg). SK Hynix planning $29B US listing to compete in AI chip market; TSMC Citi upgrade signals 2026 guidance raise on AI demand. Memory pricing strength validates earnings momentum vs. speculation.

factors.name: Hyperscaler Capex Momentum
factors.summary: Anthropic committing $15B USD for 1.4-GW Australian data centre capacity (AFR), signaling sustained cloud/AI infrastructure investment. Foxconn Q2 revenue surges on AI boost despite geopolitical warnings. Forward guidance on capex will be critical watch ahead of earnings.

factors.name: Fed Rate Expectations Easing
factors.summary: Gold prices rising and rate-hike bets cooling (per Bloomberg Markets). Reduced expectations for Fed hikes support long-duration growth multiples critical to NAS100. Real yields have likely compressed, benefiting tech valuations.

factors.name: Geopolitical De-escalation (Iran-US Deal Holds)
factors.summary: Strait of Hormuz reopening continues; tankers transiting via US-protected corridor. Oil prices collapsing on overstocking (per TotalEnergies CEO), reducing stagflation risk and supporting risk-on sentiment. De-escalation trades favor equities.

factors.name: China Macro Softness & Yuan Weakness
factors.summary: PBOC set USD/CNY reference at 6.8066 vs prior 6.7780, signaling yuan weakness. China's slower domestic growth and Beijing signalling openness to foreign capital suggest growth concerns. Heavy NAS100 exposure to China-dependent tech could drag.

factors.name: Asian Chip Stock Pullback After Rebound
factors.summary: Japan, South Korea chipmakers pulling back after recent rebound (per MarketWatch). Samsung/SK Hynix leveraged ETF volatility warnings from Bank of Korea signal potential regulatory tightening and profit-taking risk.

factors.name: Short-Term Momentum & Valuation Stretch
factors.summary: Strategist warns momentum trades face violent unwind risk in July (MarketWatch). NAS100 +0.44% in 4h after -1.24% 24h suggests choppy price action. Mega-cap tech multiples already priced for AI growth; limited margin of safety.

factors.name: Dollar Strength & Real Yields Neutral-to-Firm
factors.summary: USD/JPY at 161.70, JGB 10-yr yields 2.79% (30-yr high). Euro consolidating below 1.1400. If real yields stabilize or tick higher, long-duration growth stocks face headwind; if yields continue lower, equities rallied harder.

factors.name: Limited Near-Term Data Catalyst
factors.summary: No major CPI, Fed minutes, or employment data in immediate 24-72h window. Bond auctions (10/30Y Treasuries) this week are moderate-risk. Earnings season still ramping; NVDA/MSFT/GOOGL/AAPL dates critical but not imminent.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is caught between two competing narratives: (1) genuine AI semiconductor earnings momentum (Samsung 18x profit jump, TSMC upgrade, hyperscaler capex acceleration) and (2) extended momentum rally facing profit-taking and geopolitical/macro headwinds. The recent 4h +0.44% bounce off a 24h -1.24% decline suggests choppy consolidation rather than directional conviction. De-escalation of Iran tensions and cooling rate-hike expectations provide tailwinds, but China's Yuan weakness, Asian chip stock pullbacks, and July seasonality warnings flag caution. Mega-cap tech valuations remain stretched, and sentiment-driven reversals in momentum trades could unwind quickly ahead of earnings season.
catalysts_ahead: NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL, AMZN earnings (mid-to-late July)
Samsung earnings confirmation of memory profit beat (July 8-9)
US jobs report (early July if scheduled)
Any escalation in Strait of Hormuz tensions or geopolitical flashpoint
ECB communications on rate path (Nagel, Moulin commentary watch)
screening.risk_warning: If Fed minutes signal renewed hawkishness or inflation resilience, real yields could spike, crushing long-duration growth multiples and triggering NAS100 selloff. Conversely, if mega-cap earnings disappoint on AI capex outlook or demand slowdown signals, even dovish Fed support may not sustain rally. China stimulus reversal, Taiwan tensions escalation, or renewed Hormuz disruption are geopolitical wildcards. Momentum unwind into July could be violent—watch single-stock gamma in NVDA/MSFT/AAPL for dealer position shifts.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2 with mixed signals. Wait for pullback to 29100 support or confirmation break above 29600 resistance before committing size. Given earnings-driven volatility ahead and extended 4h positioning, scale in rather than chase. Watch Fed minutes release for directional clarity.
